skill.name: wiki-vitepress
skill.description: Packages generated wiki Markdown into a VitePress static site with dark theme, Mermaid diagram support, click-to-zoom diagrams, and production build guidance.
skill.summary: Build a dark themed VitePress site from generated wiki Markdown.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-risk looking patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown fences, color hex values, and documentation examples. The confirmed concerns are generated client code that copies diagram HTML with innerHTML and a build workflow that runs npm install and npm run docs:build.

medium_findings.title: Generated Mermaid Zoom Uses innerHTML
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends assigning modal.innerHTML from el.outerHTML when adding click-to-zoom for Mermaid diagrams. If wiki content or Mermaid output contains unsafe HTML or SVG attributes, the generated site could preserve executable markup in the modal.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The innerHTML assignment is explicit and copies rendered diagram markup. It is a real browser XSS pattern, although the exploitability depends on the safety of the source Markdown and Mermaid rendering pipeline.

medium_findings.title: Generated Build Workflow Executes npm Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs running npm install and npm run docs:build in the generated wiki-site directory. This is expected for VitePress, but npm install can execute dependency lifecycle scripts and should be limited to trusted generated package files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution is directly documented and legitimate for the skill purpose. The risk comes from npm lifecycle behavior and generated dependency trust, not from confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Documentation
low_findings.description: Most external command detections are Markdown backticks, code fences, headings, or inline documentation rather than executable skill code. No evidence found of hidden shell execution outside the documented VitePress build step.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is visible Markdown guidance and fenced examples. The only actionable command is the documented npm build command.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Color Values
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts correspond to dark theme text and hex color values in the skill description and Mermaid styling guidance. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, or other cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines describe theme behavior, CSS, or frontmatter requirements. They do not contain cryptographic APIs or hash verification logic.

low_findings.title: Hidden Path Output Is Expected VitePress Directory
low_findings.description: The hidden file access alert points to wiki-site/.vitepress/dist/, which is the standard VitePress output directory. No evidence found of reading secrets, traversing user home directories, or accessing unrelated hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden path is part of the documented generated site structure. The context is build output, not covert filesystem access.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Lack Supporting Intent
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts map to general documentation about static site generation, Mermaid rendering, DOM timing, and build output. No evidence found of host fingerprinting, process listing, credential discovery, or environment enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are UI and documentation instructions. They do not request system inventory or collect local machine details.

dangerous_patterns.title: innerHTML Assignment From Rendered Markup
dangerous_patterns.description: The generated zoom modal copies a Mermaid element using modal.innerHTML = el.outerHTML. Generated sites should prefer DOM cloning or sanitized markup for untrusted Markdown content.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code pattern is present in the skill instructions and is a known browser injection sink. The risk level depends on upstream Markdown and Mermaid sanitization.

dangerous_patterns.title: npm Install and Build Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The generated workflow runs npm install and npm run docs:build. This is normal for VitePress but can execute package lifecycle scripts from dependencies.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and required for the build. The concern is supply-chain exposure rather than evidence of malicious commands.

content.value_statement: Generated wiki pages can be hard to browse, share, and review as loose Markdown files. This skill turns them into a dark themed VitePress site with Mermaid support and build guidance.
actual_capabilities: Defines a wiki-site directory layout for VitePress output.
Provides dark theme Mermaid variables and CSS overrides.
Adds a click-to-zoom pattern for Mermaid diagrams.
Lists Markdown post-processing rules for VitePress compatibility.
Documents the npm build command and output directory.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not include executable helper scripts.
It assumes generated Markdown pages already exist before packaging.
It does not sanitize untrusted Markdown or Mermaid content.
It depends on npm, VitePress, Vue, and Mermaid plugin behavior.

use_cases.title: Publish Engineering Wikis
use_cases.description: Convert generated architecture notes into a browsable VitePress site with navigation and diagrams.

use_cases.title: Review AI Documentation Output
use_cases.description: Package generated Markdown into a local site so reviewers can inspect structure, links, and visual diagrams.

use_cases.title: Create Project Knowledge Portals
use_cases.description: Turn repository analysis notes into a static documentation site that can be built and shared.

prompt_templates.title: Build Basic Wiki Site
prompt_templates.prompt: Package these generated wiki Markdown files into a VitePress site using the wiki-vitepress structure.

prompt_templates.title: Add Mermaid Diagram Support
prompt_templates.prompt: Create the VitePress configuration and theme files needed for dark Mermaid diagrams in this wiki site.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Production Build
prompt_templates.prompt: Post-process the Markdown pages for VitePress compatibility, then prepare the package files and build instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Harden Generated Site
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the generated VitePress wiki for unsafe HTML patterns, dependency risks, broken navigation, and Mermaid rendering issues.

output_examples.input: A folder of generated Markdown pages with architecture diagrams.
output_examples.output: A VitePress wiki layout with navigation, sidebar entries, dark Mermaid styling, and build guidance.

output_examples.input: Markdown pages that include Mermaid diagrams and Vue-sensitive generic text.
output_examples.output: Updated documentation pages with frontmatter, compatible line breaks, protected generic notation, and diagram zoom behavior.

output_examples.input: A generated wiki that needs local preview and production output.
output_examples.output: A package-ready static documentation site with clear build steps and a documented output directory.

best_practices: Review generated Markdown before building, especially if it includes HTML or Mermaid diagrams from untrusted sources.
Pin VitePress and plugin versions when packaging documentation for repeatable builds.
Test navigation, sidebar links, dark mode diagrams, and production output before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not paste unreviewed external HTML into generated wiki pages.
Do not run npm install on package files that were modified by an untrusted source.
Do not rely on CSS overrides alone when Mermaid injects inline styles.

faq.question: What does this skill build?
faq.answer: It describes how to package generated Markdown wiki pages into a VitePress static site.

faq.question: Does it generate the wiki content?
faq.answer: No. It assumes wiki Markdown files already exist and focuses on packaging them as a site.

faq.question: Does it support Mermaid diagrams?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes VitePress Mermaid plugin guidance, dark theme variables, CSS overrides, and zoom behavior.

faq.question: Can it publish the site automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents local build output, but deployment remains a separate user-controlled step.

faq.question: What should users review before building?
faq.answer: Users should review generated Markdown, package dependencies, and any embedded HTML from untrusted sources.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
